zones.label: River Source
zones.description: High in mountains or forests, rivers begin as small streams from glaciers, springs, or rain. The water here is cold, clear, and oxygen-rich.

zones.label: Tributaries
zones.description: Smaller streams join the main river, adding water, nutrients, and sediment. The river grows wider and more powerful.

zones.label: Floodplains
zones.description: The river slows down in flatter areas. It deposits sediment, recharges groundwater, and supports agriculture and settlements.

zones.label: Delta / River Mouth
zones.description: The river reaches the sea and deposits sediment, forming a network of wetlands and islands. Deltas are often rich in biodiversity.
